text.title: Champion Challenger Pattern for RAG Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the champion-challenger-pattern-rag methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Challenger arm MUST run in shadow mode: it reads the production query, computes a response, but its response MUST NOT reach any user under any failover path.
rule.rationale: Without strict no-leak, the bench becomes an A/B test on users, which violates the methodology's purpose (debate-free promotion).

rule.statement: Bench cycle MUST be a fixed 7-day window. Promotion / no-promotion decision happens on the cycle boundary; no mid-cycle promotions allowed.
rule.rationale: Fixed cadence reduces decision-fatigue and keeps champion stable long enough to gather statistically meaningful data.

rule.statement: Promotion MUST require: challenger score ≥ champion score + 3pp AND n ≥ 1000 shadow queries AND no safety regressions (zero refusal-failure / PII-leak incidents).
rule.rationale: 3pp + n=1000 sized for typical RAG noise (±1.5pp). Below either, the win is within noise.

rule.statement: After promotion, the previous champion MUST remain warm for 24h with traffic-shift rollback wired. If new champion regresses > 5pp on first 24h of user traffic, auto-rollback to previous.
rule.rationale: Shadow eval may diverge from user-facing performance due to UI/latency factors; 24h watch catches the gap.

rule.statement: Every promotion / no-promotion decision MUST be logged with: champion_id, challenger_id, judge_id, score delta, n, decision timestamp, decider. Logs are append-only.
rule.rationale: Without audit trail, ML-ops cannot reconstruct why a given version was deployed; regulatory audits require this trail.
